name: azure-ad-integration
description: Microsoft Graph API for Azure AD / Entra ID. Users, groups, devices, and directory, plus applications and service principals, OAuth consent-grant detection, usage and MFA registration reports, tenant policy posture, calendar, threat assessment and Defender Threat Intelligence, email forensics and search, and full incident-response tooling (sign-in logs, risk detections, Conditional Access, session revocation).

tenant_id.title: Tenant ID
tenant_id.description: Your Azure AD tenant ID (Directory ID). Find it in Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Overview.

client_id.title: Client (App) ID
client_id.description: App registration client ID. IMPORTANT: All permissions must be Application type (not Delegated) with admin consent granted — this app uses client credentials (no user sign-in). Microsoft Graph Application permissions needed: AuditLog.Read.All, Directory.Read.All, IdentityRiskyUser.ReadWrite.All, IdentityRiskEvent.ReadWrite.All, Mail.Read (Application — required to read any user's mailbox), MailboxSettings.Read, Policy.Read.All, Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess, ThreatHunting.Read.All, User.Read.All, User.RevokeSessions.All, UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All. Also needs Office 365 Management APIs: ActivityFeed.Read. NOTE: Mail.Read as Delegated (not Application) will only allow access to one mailbox and cause ErrorItemNotFound on all others.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: App registration client secret. Create one in Azure Portal → App registrations → Certificates & secrets.
